narrative.headline: 2018 Chevrolet Camaro: electrical and steering lead owner complaints
narrative.summary: Across 77 owner complaints to NHTSA, the electrical system is the most-reported area at 20 reports (26%), followed by steering and power train, tied at 16 reports each (21%). Most complaints did not involve a crash or injury. The electrical system reports include 2 fires, and the power train category has the only crash and injury in this data.

issues.sample: IF THE CAR IS NOT KEPT ON A CHARGING SYSTEM THE BATTERY CAN DRAIN WITHIN 48 HOURS - 2 WEEKS. WHEN I LEAVE THE CAR IN THE GARAGE WITH THE DOORS LOCKED FOR 48 HOURS THE BATTERY GOES DEAD. WHEN I LEAVE IT IN THE GARAGE FOR A WEEK UNLOCKED THE BATTERY IS DEAD. IT TOOK 45 MINUTES TO J
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Most-reported area: 20 complaints (26%), including 2 fires, with no crashes or injuries reported. Owners describe the battery draining and going dead after the car sits for a few days.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: 16 complaints (21%), with no crashes or injuries reported. Owners describe a "Service Power Steering" warning appearing while driving.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 16 complaints (21%), and the only category here with a crash and an injury (1 each). Owners report a grinding noise from the rear end, especially when the car is cold or turning.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 15 complaints (19%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Owners describe the car cutting engine power and bogging down or stalling when taking off from a stop.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: 6 complaints (8%), including 1 fire, with no crashes or injuries reported. One owner describes a fire that started inside the car and spread to the seat.
